texto: When checking in at an airport, no matter how rude the check-in person is to you, always smile and be nice because you don't know what kind of day they've had. You are going on holiday and they're stuck wherever they are. Be nice to them because they can re-route your baggage to wherever they feel like.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ross Kemp: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “When checking in at an airport, no matter how rude the check-in person is to you, always smile an…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, day, holiday pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de holiday e nice.
